records.ety: [F. ponctuel (cf. Sp. puntual, It. puntuale), from L. punctum point. See Point.]

defs: Consisting in a point; limited to a point; unextended.
quotes: The theory of the punctual existence of the soul.

defs: Observant of nice points; punctilious; precise.
quotes: Punctual to tediousness in all that he relates.
So much on punctual niceties they stand.

defs: Appearing or done at, or adhering exactly to, a regular or an appointed time; precise; prompt; as, a punctual man; a punctual payment.
quotes: These sharp strokes [of a pendulum], with their inexorably steady intersections, so agree with our successive thoughts that they seem like the punctual stops counting off our very souls into the past.
